title: Camp David

jokes.text: Which one of you Johnny Appledick shit for brains came up with that bit?
jokes.setup: Someone created a campaign bit involving an apple that failed
jokes.punchline: Selina's profane wordplay on 'Johnny Appleseed' with sexual/insulting twist
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina Meyer profanity-laced insult with creative wordplay. Shows her character perfectly - crude, angry, creative with language.

jokes.text: Hi, ma'am, I just arrived here in Winter Blunderland.
jokes.setup: Jonah calling in from New Hampshire in winter
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'Winter Wonderland' to indicate his political blundering
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Jonah's self-aware incompetence, but predictable wordplay.

jokes.text: He looked like Ike Turner handing Tina a snack.
jokes.setup: Describing how Jonah looked handing an apple to Mrs. Sherman
jokes.punchline: Darkly compares campaign moment to domestic abuse scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected comparison. Economy of language is perfect, though edges into offensive territory.

jokes.text: I think we've identified the key problem, which is that Jonah does not interact well with humans.
jokes.setup: Analyzing Jonah's campaign performance issues
jokes.punchline: Deadpan suggestion that Jonah's problem is basic human interaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of obvious truth about Jonah's social incompetence.

jokes.text: No, I don't interact well with hag-ass old bitches.
jokes.setup: Previous line about Jonah not interacting well with humans
jokes.punchline: Jonah's crude, politically disastrous response proving the point
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah - proving the criticism right with crude language. Character-perfect but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Do you realize that you just handed O'Brien New Hampshire chained to a radiator with its twat shaved?
jokes.setup: Jonah's disastrous campaign performance
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely vivid metaphor for political damage
jokes.explanation: Shockingly vivid and disturbing metaphor that's both hilarious and appalling. Classic Veep grotesquery.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Mom.
jokes.setup: Selina screaming profanely at Jonah
jokes.punchline: Jonah responds like a scolded child calling her 'Mom'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Jonah's infantile response to authority. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Well, then why is Gary here?
jokes.setup: Selina saying it's just family for the weekend
jokes.punchline: Andrew points out Gary's questionable family status
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective jab at Gary's ambiguous position in Selina's life.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm excited to get to know fun Marjorie, too. When is she arriving?
jokes.setup: Marjorie talking about showing her 'fun' side
jokes.punchline: Selina sarcastically implies current Marjorie isn't fun
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina passive-aggressive dig delivered as fake enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I'm just kidding. I'm just teasing.
jokes.setup: Her sarcastic comment about fun Marjorie
jokes.punchline: Awkward backpedal that doesn't help
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment of failed damage control. Not brilliant but realistic awkwardness.

jokes.text: So you're gonna have to keep Charles Ponzi and pussyride away from me.
jokes.setup: Need to keep family away during Chinese meeting
jokes.punchline: Insulting nicknames for Andrew and Catherine
jokes.explanation: Creative insulting nicknames that capture both characters. Crude but inventive.

jokes.text: Popcornaments!
jokes.setup: Monica describing her cranberry popcorn decoration
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic portmanteau of popcorn and ornaments
jokes.explanation: Cute but predictable portmanteau. Shows Monica's crafty enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Someone's been reading my blog. I just trademarked the word last week.
jokes.setup: Someone else saying 'Popcornaments'
jokes.punchline: Monica's claim to have trademarked this obvious word
jokes.explanation: Absurd overprotectiveness of a simple portmanteau. Good character work.

jokes.text: It sounds delicious and it's adorable. So I'm gonna call it delorable.
jokes.setup: Monica's pride in her portmanteau
jokes.punchline: Selina mockingly creates her own portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dismissive mockery. Shows how Selina deflates others' enthusiasm with superior wordplay.

jokes.text: See? We can all do it.
jokes.setup: Creating her own portmanteau to mock Monica's
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about the ease of wordplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina deflation of others' pride. Economy and character integration are excellent.

jokes.text: Oh, so that's two things she's turned you into.
jokes.setup: Catherine saying Marjorie turned her into a raw vegan
jokes.punchline: Selina implying Marjorie also turned her gay
jokes.explanation: Sharp, cutting implication delivered with perfect economy. Classic Selina passive-aggression.

jokes.text: Yeah, we just thought maybe we could have, like, a sham ham for Christmas.
jokes.setup: Discussing raw vegan alternatives for Christmas dinner
jokes.punchline: 'Sham ham' as fake ham alternative
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits Catherine's earnest but naive character.

jokes.text: No, ma'am, tofurkey is cooked. This is a raw log made of mushrooms and soaked walnuts.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if sham ham is like tofurkey
jokes.punchline: Description of unappetizing raw food creation
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor from describing unappetizing health food. Fits character well.

jokes.text: But it tastes like a ham? - Yeah, it's close. - Not at all.
jokes.setup: Question about whether the raw log tastes like ham
jokes.punchline: Catherine's optimism immediately contradicted by Marjorie
jokes.explanation: Nice quick contradiction that shows Catherine's self-deception and Marjorie's honesty.

jokes.text: I thought you were Selina. - That's okay, sir. People make that mistake constantly.
jokes.setup: Andrew apologizing for mistaking Marjorie for Selina
jokes.punchline: Marjorie's deadpan claim this happens constantly
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery suggesting either she's delusional or people don't pay attention.

jokes.text: How about you touch neither of us?
jokes.setup: Andrew's awkward interaction with both women
jokes.punchline: Selina's sharp boundary-setting
jokes.explanation: Sharp, efficient shutdown of Andrew's awkwardness. Perfect Selina.

jokes.text: Okay, well, that's terrifying, but I actually do think we need to get some wood from outside.
jokes.setup: Marjorie's romantic comment about Catherine's eyes
jokes.punchline: Selina's honest reaction while trying to move on
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to awkward moment - acknowledges the weirdness while moving forward.

jokes.text: Presumably driving slowly and not using their signals.
jokes.setup: Chinese delegation delayed on the road
jokes.punchline: Gary's casual racist stereotype about Asian driving
jokes.explanation: Lazy stereotype humor that lands because of Gary's casual delivery, but not clever.

jokes.text: What do you got going there, Depravey Crockett?
jokes.setup: Someone (presumably Mike) whittling or carving
jokes.punchline: Nickname playing on 'Davy Crockett' with 'deprave'
jokes.explanation: Creative insulting nickname that suggests both frontier skills and perversion.

jokes.text: You can't go in with a plan. The knife tells you.
jokes.setup: Question about what he's carving
jokes.punchline: Mike's pseudo-philosophical approach to whittling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike line - pretentious pseudo-wisdom about something trivial. Very quotable.

jokes.text: How is it that that Asperger salad inserted herself into this situation?
jokes.setup: Minna Häkkinen arriving for the negotiations
jokes.punchline: Offensive nickname combining 'Asperger's' with 'asparagus'
jokes.explanation: Crude but creative insult. Offensive but fits Selina's character of inappropriate nicknames.

jokes.text: No, I'll tell you what he likes. Nordic beav.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting President Lu trusts Minna for her work
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude sexual explanation for the preference
jokes.explanation: Crude but efficient. Shows Selina reducing everything to base motivations.

jokes.text: And you changed your eyelids. You had oculoplasty.
jokes.setup: Minna commenting on Selina's hair highlights
jokes.punchline: Minna's blunt observation about plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment of inappropriate honesty about appearance.

jokes.text: You look more alert.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Selina's eyelid surgery
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about looking 'alert'
jokes.explanation: Perfect backhanded compliment that suggests she looked sleepy before.

jokes.text: Yeah, surround you with some trees. Make you look human-sized.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to keep Selina away from people for polls
jokes.punchline: Trees will make her look normal-sized (implying she's unusually small)
jokes.explanation: Sharp visual joke about Selina's height using trees as scale reference.

jokes.text: Yeah, back to the natural habitat your nitwit mother found you in, you fucking shaved Sasquatch.
jokes.setup: Previous comment about surrounding with trees
jokes.punchline: Extended insult comparing Jonah to a found forest creature
jokes.explanation: Creative extended insult with good escalation and vivid imagery.

jokes.text: Does she choke for Coke?
jokes.setup: Jonah asking about Minna's 'situation'
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual pun about oral sex
jokes.explanation: Crude but economical sexual pun. Fits Jonah's crude character but not clever.

jokes.text: You're menopausal as well. - No, I'm not. - Well... - What? No, you are.
jokes.setup: Minna sharing about her menopause
jokes.punchline: Awkward insistence that Selina is also menopausal
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation about age and biology. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: No, I am actually two years younger.
jokes.setup: Selina suggesting Minna is older
jokes.punchline: Minna's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan correction that makes the situation more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Amex gave me this crazy new card that's made out of black metal.
jokes.setup: Talking about the special pen
jokes.punchline: Catherine's casual mention of exclusive credit card
jokes.explanation: Shows Catherine's oblivious wealth privilege in casual aside.

jokes.text: Stuck in security. I don't know how that happened. - I got the tracking number.
jokes.setup: Pretending Gary has a gift for Marjorie stuck in security
jokes.punchline: Gary's desperate attempt to support the lie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gary's loyalty to Selina's lies.

jokes.text: That's the kitchen crew and the chef. They wear really nice suits.
jokes.setup: Catherine's observation about Asian people in golf carts
jokes.punchline: Gary's explanation that kitchen staff wear formal attire
jokes.explanation: Gary's attempt to rationalize the formal dress code for kitchen workers adds to the confusion.

jokes.text: I noticed two golf carts filled with Asian people. - Yeah, we should go. - That's the kitchen crew and the chef.
jokes.setup: Catherine reporting seeing Asian people
jokes.punchline: It's just the kitchen staff, not the Chinese delegation
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke about mistaking kitchen staff for diplomats.

jokes.text: Thank you. I didn't get any of that except strawberries.
jokes.setup: Minna giving detailed introductions of Chinese officials
jokes.punchline: Selina's honest admission she understood nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina honesty about her ignorance, delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: It looks just like my daughter Catherine.
jokes.setup: Receiving gift of cartoon pig from Chinese
jokes.punchline: Selina comparing her daughter to a sad piglet
jokes.explanation: Mortifyingly inappropriate comment that's perfectly in character for Selina.

jokes.text: Your daughter is a beautiful woman and this is a piglet. - It's upset.
jokes.setup: Lu pointing out the inappropriateness of the comparison
jokes.punchline: Selina doubling down by focusing on the pig's emotion
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the cringe with Selina making it worse by doubling down.

jokes.text: Believe me, if you say SeaWorld to Catherine you will understand what I'm talking about.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain the pig comparison
jokes.punchline: Reference to Catherine's presumably traumatic SeaWorld experience
jokes.explanation: Mysterious reference that suggests some humiliating Catherine incident. Good character detail.

jokes.text: Thank you for this rock.
jokes.setup: Selina's elaborate description of the American geode gift
jokes.punchline: Lu's blunt, unimpressed summary
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Selina's pretentious gift description. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: which is local to us and it's also close to Asia, which makes it local to you as we are all local friends.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain New Zealand wine as a gift
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate logic about 'local' geography
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Selina's verbal flailing when improvising explanations.

jokes.text: They say you look like a prisoner or a pirate.
jokes.setup: Chinese discussing Selina's casual dress
jokes.punchline: Two unflattering comparisons for the President
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment of diplomatic feedback about appearance.

jokes.text: Jonah shot himself in the eye with a rubber band in second grade.
jokes.setup: Jonah literally shooting himself in the foot
jokes.punchline: Precedent of childhood self-injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail establishing pattern of Jonah's self-inflicted accidents.

jokes.text: No, not for him, for me.
jokes.setup: Being told about Jonah's complicated fracture recovery
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she only cares about political impact on herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina selfishness - redirecting concern from injured person to personal consequences.

jokes.text: Or rolls, I should say.
jokes.setup: Mentioning FDR's morning strolls
jokes.punchline: Selina correcting to reference his wheelchair
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical joke about FDR's disability. Inappropriate but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Except without any food.
jokes.setup: Saying North Koreans are people like us
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to North Korean starvation
jokes.explanation: Dark but efficient joke about serious humanitarian crisis. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: My family is here as a cover story.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Catherine didn't recognize President Lu
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that family is just for show
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty about using family as political props. Perfect Selina character moment.

jokes.text: Feels like it was woven out of Catherine's silken hair. Feel this.
jokes.setup: Marjorie examining the scarf gift
jokes.punchline: Creepy romantic comparison to Catherine's hair
jokes.explanation: Appropriately creepy romantic gesture that makes everyone uncomfortable.

jokes.text: It's the outfit I picture when I'm trying not to come.
jokes.setup: Commenting on someone's pajamas
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual confession about mental imagery
jokes.explanation: Shockingly crude but well-delivered line. Good character moment for Ben's crudeness.

jokes.text: It's the NRA. It's a Christmas miracle.
jokes.setup: NRA ad attacking gun control advocate who shot himself
jokes.punchline: Calling NRA support a 'Christmas miracle'
jokes.explanation: Good irony about finding political salvation in gun lobby support.

jokes.text: Then why is my wonton soup so cold every time they deliver it?
jokes.setup: Minna saying Chinese are sensitive to promptness
jokes.punchline: Selina's non-sequitur about Chinese food delivery
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist non-sequitur that shows Selina's scattered thinking under pressure.

jokes.text: Sort of an intermezzo emergency.
jokes.setup: Claiming Kuwait emergency happened after dinner
jokes.punchline: Using musical term 'intermezzo' for timing
jokes.explanation: Clever use of musical terminology for diplomatic crisis timing. Shows Selina's vocabulary.

jokes.text: This happens occasionally in Iceland, but there it's just an accident.
jokes.setup: Discussion of diplomatic incidents
jokes.punchline: Minna's matter-of-fact comparison to Icelandic accidents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Minna deadpan delivery of absurd international comparison.

jokes.text: It's nicotine gum.
jokes.setup: Selina asking for more of the amazing Chinese gum
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's just nicotine replacement therapy
jokes.explanation: Deflating Selina's enthusiasm for exotic gum by revealing it's mundane smoking cessation aid.

jokes.text: Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Learning the Chinese gum is nicotine gum
jokes.punchline: Selina's delayed realization of addiction implications
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Economy is perfect for the realization.

jokes.text: I mean, Daddy loves you, sweetie, but he's a crook.
jokes.setup: Warning Catherine about taking financial advice from Andrew
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact assessment of ex-husband's character
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of harsh truth. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: You have a very narrow vagina? - Well, tight. In the States we say tight.
jokes.setup: Discussion of difficult childbirth
jokes.punchline: Awkward international terminology correction
jokes.explanation: Appropriately awkward medical discussion with cultural terminology differences.

jokes.text: Like a Chinese Mr. Darcy. But stupid also, like a Chinese Mr. Bingley.
jokes.setup: Describing President Lu's character
jokes.punchline: Jane Austen character comparison with ethnic modifier
jokes.explanation: Clever literary comparison with good escalation from pride to stupidity.

jokes.text: I love 'Mary Poppins.'
jokes.setup: Minna's Jane Austen references
jokes.punchline: Selina's completely wrong cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment of cultural ignorance delivered confidently.

jokes.text: Bono's gonna shit his sunglasses.
jokes.setup: Discussion of freeing Tibet
jokes.punchline: Crude prediction of Bono's reaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and highly quotable line. Perfect Ben crudeness with creative imagery.

jokes.text: Worry about your own unemployment in Finland. - We don't have unemployment in Finland.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to insult Minna about unemployment
jokes.punchline: Minna's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Selina's attempted insult with Nordic social democracy facts.

jokes.text: Pow, pow. Who should I vote for? You can't teach that.
jokes.setup: Jonah at voting booth with his injured foot
jokes.punchline: Making shooting gestures while referencing his accident
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jonah's obliviousness to irony of making gun gestures.

jokes.text: Freedom log.
jokes.setup: Presenting the vegan ham substitute at Christmas dinner
jokes.punchline: Rebranding the 'sham ham' with patriotic terminology
jokes.explanation: Selina's patriotic rebranding of the vegan ham, linking it to Tibet liberation excitement.

jokes.text: Your adoption paper has been approved. You and your wife soon will have a Chinese baby.
jokes.setup: Formal diplomatic farewell
jokes.punchline: Completely random adoption announcement
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected non-sequitur that's brilliantly absurd and mysterious.

jokes.text: Oh, H.R. Fucknstuf, Jonah won the election?
jokes.setup: Learning Jonah won his Congressional race
jokes.punchline: Creative profane nickname for H.R. Pufnstuf
jokes.explanation: Creative profane wordplay on children's TV character. Classic Selina insulting nickname.

jokes.text: I can elect a Muslim AIDS virus. A terrorist fucking AIDS virus in the tiniest suicide vest ever made, I could make it governor of New Hampshire.
jokes.setup: Jonah boasting about his electoral power
jokes.punchline: Increasingly offensive hypothetical candidate description
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah escalation into offensive territory. Character-appropriate but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Like Jessica Thompson. Hey, Jessica. Jessica, I see you there. Yeah, it's been a long time since high school. I like what you did with your hair. Do you like what I did with my life?
jokes.setup: Jonah giving victory speech
jokes.punchline: Petty high school revenge moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah pettiness in moment of triumph. Good character escalation.

jokes.text: You're taking my daddy's house and turning it into some Club Med for goats?
jokes.setup: Catherine wanting to make house an animal sanctuary
jokes.punchline: Reducing it to vacation resort for goats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reduction of animal sanctuary to luxury resort concept.

tmdb_overview: Selina takes Catherine to a family pre-Christmas celebration at Camp David, where her team also plans to conduct secret negotiations. After a disastrous debate in New Hampshire, Amy and Dan try to make Jonah seem more human.
insight: Political chaos erupts at 2.08 jokes per minute—Veep's fastest-paced episode yet.
